_meta.scope: High-frequency sections across IPC->BNS, CrPC->BNSS, Evidence->BSA. Curated core, NOT exhaustive. Extend as needed.
sources: BNS/IPC concordance - UP Police official comparative table (uppolice.gov.in, Three New Major Acts)
CrPC/BNSS comparison - Bureau of Police Research & Development (bprd.nic.in)
BSA/Evidence concordance - UP Police & NCRB CyTrain (cytrain.ncrb.gov.in)
_meta.verification_status: Section-number mappings cross-checked against the official government concordances above (high confidence). Punishment and First-Schedule classification fields (cognizable / bailable / compoundable / court) are compiled best-effort and should be verified against the bare Act + BNSS First Schedule by the advocate before relying in court. Entries needing that check carry confidence="verify-classification".
_meta.disclaimer: Reference aid, not legal advice. Numbering and classification can be amended; verify against the current bare Act before filing.

change_type_legend.renumbered: Same offence/provision, new number, no material change
change_type_legend.substantive: Number changed AND punishment/classification/scope materially changed
change_type_legend.new: New offence/provision with no IPC/CrPC/Evidence ancestor
change_type_legend.omitted: No direct successor in the new code
change_type_legend.merged: Multiple old sections consolidated into one new section

mappings.summary: Definition of culpable homicide renumbered 299->100. Substantively unchanged.
Definition of murder renumbered 300->101. Substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 302->103. Punishment, cognizability and bail unchanged. NOTE: BNS 103(2) separately adds murder by a group of 5+ on identity grounds (mob lynching) - a new offence.

punishment.old: Death, or imprisonment for life, + fine
punishment.new: Death, or imprisonment for life, + fine

old.title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder

new.title: Punishment for culpable homicide not amounting to murder

mappings.summary: Renumbered 304->105. Two-part punishment structure retained. Verify exact terms of imprisonment against bare Act.

punishment.old: Life, or up to 10 yrs + fine (Part I); up to 10 yrs or fine or both (Part II)
punishment.new: Life, or 5-10 yrs + fine (Part I); up to 10 yrs or fine or both (Part II)

mappings.summary: PUNISHMENT INCREASED: max imprisonment raised from 2 yrs to 5 yrs. Registered medical practitioner doing the act in good faith capped at 2 yrs. BNS 106(2) NEWLY adds hit-and-run-and-flee (causing death by rash driving and escaping without reporting) - up to 10 yrs + fine.

punishment.old: Up to 2 yrs, or fine, or both
punishment.new: Up to 5 yrs + fine (2 yrs for registered medical practitioner); 106(2) hit-and-run-flee up to 10 yrs + fine

mappings.summary: Renumbered 304B->80. Ingredients and punishment unchanged.

punishment.old: Min 7 yrs to imprisonment for life
punishment.new: Min 7 yrs to imprisonment for life

punishment.new: Up to 10 yrs + fine

punishment.new: Up to 10 yrs + fine; life if hurt caused

mappings.summary: Renumbered 308->110. Substantively unchanged.

punishment.new: Imprisonment up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both; if hurt is caused, up to 7 yrs, or fine, or both

Renumbered 323->115(2). Punishment unchanged (fine amount updated).

punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both

punishment.new: Up to 7 yrs + fine

punishment.new: Life, or up to 10 yrs + fine

punishment.new: Min 10 yrs to life + fine (for victim treatment)

punishment.new: 1 to 5 yrs + fine

punishment.new: Clauses (i)-(iii): up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both. Clause (iv): up to 1 yr, or fine, or both

punishment.new: 1st conviction: up to 3 yrs + fine. 2nd/subsequent: up to 5 yrs + fine

punishment.old: Up to 10 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Up to 10 yrs + fine

Renumbered 376->64 (your existing _BNS_MAP said 63 - that is the definition section; 64 is punishment). Punishment unchanged.

punishment.old: Min 10 yrs to life + fine

mappings.summary: Renumbered 509->79.

punishment.old: Simple imp. up to 3 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Simple imp. up to 3 yrs + fine

mappings.summary: OMITTED. BNS has NO direct equivalent of IPC 377. After Navtej Johar (2018) consensual same-sex acts were already decriminalised, but the omission leaves a debated gap for non-consensual unnatural acts against men/animals. Treat with care - subject of representations and pending consideration.
Renumbered 363->137(2). Kidnapping defined in 137(1).

punishment.old: Up to 7 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Up to 7 yrs + fine

mappings.summary: Definition of theft renumbered 378->303(1).

Renumbered 379->303(2). NEW: for first-time petty theft (value < Rs 5,000) where property is returned/restored, punishment may be COMMUNITY SERVICE - a new penalty type introduced by BNS. Otherwise up to 3 yrs or fine or both.

punishment.old: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both
punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both; community service for first-time petty theft (<Rs 5,000) on return of property

new.title: Theft in dwelling house, transport or place of worship

mappings.summary: Renumbered 380->305. Scope expanded to expressly include means of transport and places of worship.

punishment.old: Up to 7 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Up to 7 yrs + fine

punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both

mappings.summary: Renumbered 420->318(4). The famous '420' is now 318(4). Punishment unchanged.

punishment.old: Up to 7 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Up to 7 yrs + fine

compoundable.old: Yes, with court permission (by person cheated)
compoundable.new: Yes, with court permission (by person cheated)

Renumbered 447->329(3). Criminal trespass / house-trespass defined in 329(1)-(2).

punishment.new: Up to 3 months, or fine up to Rs 5,000, or both

Renumbered 465->336(2).

punishment.new: Up to 2 yrs, or fine, or both

punishment.old: Up to 7 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Up to 7 yrs + fine

new.title: Using as genuine a forged document or electronic record

mappings.summary: Renumbered 471->340(2).
Renumbered 489A->178.

punishment.old: Life, or up to 10 yrs + fine
punishment.new: Life, or up to 10 yrs + fine

mappings.summary: Defamation renumbered 499->356. Punishment 500->356(2). NEW: community service is added as a possible punishment for defamation.
Renumbered 500->356(2). NEW: community service added as a punishment option alongside imprisonment/fine.

punishment.old: Simple imp. up to 2 yrs, or fine, or both
punishment.new: Simple imp. up to 2 yrs, or fine, or both, or community service

court.old: Magistrate, First Class / Court of Session
court.new: Magistrate, First Class / Court of Session

mappings.summary: Criminal intimidation renumbered 503->351. Punishment 506->351(2)/(3).
Renumbered 506->351(2) (up to 2 yrs); aggravated threat (death/grievous hurt/fire) is 351(3) (up to 7 yrs).

punishment.old: Up to 2 yrs / fine; up to 7 yrs if threat of death, grievous hurt, etc.
punishment.new: Up to 2 yrs / fine; up to 7 yrs if threat of death, grievous hurt, etc.

new.title: Act endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India

mappings.summary: NOT a clean swap. 'Sedition' as a label is GONE. BNS 152 creates a differently-framed offence targeting acts (incl. by electronic/financial means) exciting secession, armed rebellion, subversive activities, or endangering sovereignty/unity/integrity. Scope and ingredients differ from old sedition - pre-2024 sedition case law applies only with caution.

punishment.old: Life or up to 3 yrs + fine (124A)
punishment.new: Life, or up to 7 yrs, + fine (152)

old.title: Promoting enmity between groups on grounds of religion, race, etc.

new.title: Promoting enmity between groups on grounds of religion, race, etc.

mappings.summary: Renumbered 153A->196.

punishment.old: Up to 3 yrs (5 yrs if in place of worship) + fine
punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs (5 yrs if in place of worship) + fine

old.title: Deliberate acts to outrage religious feelings

new.title: Deliberate acts to outrage religious feelings

mappings.summary: Renumbered 295A->299. (Note: BNS 299 is the religious-feelings offence; do not confuse with IPC 299 culpable homicide, which is now BNS 100.)

punishment.old: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both
punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs, or fine, or both

old.title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation

new.title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation

mappings.summary: Renumbered 352->131 (matches your reference image). Assault defined 351->130; criminal force 350->129.

punishment.old: Up to 3 months, or fine up to Rs 500, or both
punishment.new: Up to 3 months, or fine up to Rs 1,000, or both

old.title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention

new.title: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention

mappings.summary: Common intention renumbered 34->3(5).
Conspiracy renumbered 120A/120B -> 61(1)/61(2).
Renumbered 149->190. Unlawful assembly defined 141->189.

old.title: No IPC equivalent (was covered only by State MCOCA-type laws)

mappings.summary: NEW OFFENCE. Brings organised crime (syndicate activity: kidnapping, extortion, contract killing, cyber-crime, trafficking, economic offences) into the general penal code for the first time. Death/life if it causes death; otherwise min 5 yrs to life + fine.

punishment.new: If it causes death: death or imprisonment for life + fine of at least Rs. 10 lakh. Otherwise: 5 yrs to imprisonment for life + fine of at least Rs. 5 lakh

mappings.summary: NEW OFFENCE. Targets organised pickpocketing, snatching, theft, betting/gambling rings, sale of exam papers, etc. by gangs. 1 to 7 yrs + fine.

punishment.new: 1 to 7 yrs + fine

mappings.summary: NEW in the general penal code. Defines terrorist act (threat to unity/integrity/security/economic security, or to overawe govt / intimidate public). Death/life if it causes death; else min 5 yrs to life. NOTE: UAPA continues to apply in parallel - investigating officer (rank SP+) decides which to invoke.

punishment.new: If it causes death: death, or imprisonment for life without parole, + fine. Otherwise: 5 yrs to imprisonment for life, + fine

old.title: No standalone IPC offence (charged as theft/robbery)

mappings.summary: NEW standalone offence. Snatching (sudden/quick/forcible seizure) is now distinct from theft. Up to 3 yrs + fine. (Earlier such acts were charged as theft u/IPC 379 or robbery.)

punishment.new: Up to 3 yrs + fine

new.title: Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means / false promise

mappings.summary: NEW OFFENCE. Sexual intercourse by deceitful means (incl. false promise of marriage, employment, promotion, or marrying by suppressing identity) without amounting to rape. Up to 10 yrs + fine. Earlier prosecuted, contentiously, under IPC 375/376 or 417/420.

punishment.new: Up to 10 yrs + fine

old.title: No specific IPC offence (charged under 302 r/w 34/149)

new.title: Murder by group on grounds of identity (mob lynching)

mappings.summary: NEW. Murder by a group of 5+ persons on grounds of race, caste, community, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief, etc. Death, or life imprisonment, + fine.

punishment.new: Death, or imprisonment for life, + fine (for each member of the group)

old.title: When police may arrest without warrant

new.title: When police may arrest without warrant

mappings.summary: Renumbered 41->35. NEW safeguard in 35(7): for offences punishable up to 3 yrs, a person who is infirm or aged 60+ cannot be arrested without prior approval of an officer of at least DSP rank.

old.title: Notice of appearance before police officer

new.title: Notice of appearance before police officer

mappings.summary: CrPC 41A notice (Arnesh Kumar safeguard) now sits in BNSS 35(3)-(6).

old.title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds and right to bail

new.title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds and right to bail

mappings.summary: Renumbered 50->47.

old.title: Examination of arrested person by medical officer

new.title: Examination of arrested person by medical officer

mappings.summary: Renumbered 54->53.

old.title: Summons to produce document or other thing

new.title: Summons to produce document or other thing (incl. electronic)

mappings.summary: Renumbered 91->94. Expressly extended to electronic communication / digital evidence and devices.

Renumbered 154->173. MAJOR CHANGES: (a) Zero FIR is now statutory - FIR can be registered at ANY police station irrespective of jurisdiction; (b) e-FIR / information by electronic means is recognised (to be signed within 3 days); (c) for offences punishable 3-7 yrs, a preliminary enquiry (within 14 days) is permitted with DSP+ approval before registering FIR.

old.title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case; 156(3) Magistrate direction

new.title: Police power to investigate; 175(3) Magistrate direction

mappings.summary: Renumbered 156->175 (your _BNS_MAP correctly has 156(3)->175(3)). NEW in 175(3): before ordering investigation the Magistrate must consider the complainant's application and may require the police to first submit a report; aimed at curbing frivolous 156(3) directions.
Renumbered 161->180. Statements may now be recorded by audio-video electronic means.

old.title: Recording of confessions and statements by Magistrate

new.title: Recording of confessions and statements by Magistrate

mappings.summary: Renumbered 164->183. Audio-video recording encouraged; provisions for statements of vulnerable victims retained.

old.title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in 24 hours (remand)

new.title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in 24 hours (remand)

mappings.summary: Renumbered 167->187. MAJOR CHANGE: the 15-day police-custody limit may now be sought in parts within the first 40 days (offences < 10 yrs) or 60 days (offences >= 10 yrs / death) of the investigation period - rather than only within the first 15 days. Default-bail thresholds (60/90 days) are retained.

old.title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation (charge sheet)

new.title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation (charge sheet)

mappings.summary: Renumbered 173->193. NEW: investigation in rape cases to be completed within 2 months; status of investigation to be informed to complainant within 90 days; charge sheet must be forwarded electronically; supplementary investigation u/193(9) with court permission.

old.title: Maintenance of wives, children and parents

new.title: Maintenance of wives, children and parents

mappings.summary: Renumbered 125->144. Substantively unchanged - note the new number clashes with old CrPC 144 (prohibitory orders), now BNSS 163, so be careful which '144' is meant.

old.title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger

new.title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger

mappings.summary: The famous 'Section 144' (prohibitory orders) is now BNSS 163. Substantively unchanged.
Renumbered 190->210.

Renumbered 200->223. NEW proviso: a Magistrate cannot take cognizance on a complaint without giving the accused an opportunity of being heard; and complaints against public servants for acts in discharge of duty require a report from the superior officer.
Renumbered 204->227. (CrPC 202 postponement -> BNSS 225; CrPC 203 dismissal -> BNSS 226.)
Renumbered 227->250. NEW: the accused may move a discharge application within 60 days from the date of commitment of the case.
Renumbered 313->351. (Note: BNS 351 is criminal intimidation; BNSS 351 is examination of accused - different codes.)
Renumbered 320->359. The table of compoundable offences is re-cast in terms of BNS section numbers - check the new table, not the IPC-based one.
Renumbered 372->413 (matches your _BNS_MAP). Victim's proviso right of appeal retained.

old.title: Maximum period of detention of undertrial prisoner

new.title: Maximum period of detention of undertrial prisoner

mappings.summary: Renumbered 436A->479. NEW relief: a first-time offender (no prior conviction) is released on bond after serving 1/3 of the maximum sentence; others after 1/2. Excludes offences punishable with life/death. Jail Superintendent must apply for release once the threshold is met.

old.title: When bail may be taken in non-bailable offence (Magistrate)

new.title: When bail may be taken in non-bailable offence (Magistrate)

mappings.summary: Renumbered 437->480 (matches your _BNS_MAP).
Anticipatory bail renumbered 438->482 (matches your _BNS_MAP). The CrPC 438(1) guiding-factors sub-clauses were dropped in BNSS 482, restoring wider judicial discretion.

old.title: Special powers of High Court / Court of Session regarding bail

new.title: Special powers of High Court / Court of Session regarding bail

mappings.summary: Renumbered 439->483 (matches your _BNS_MAP).
Inherent powers (quashing) renumbered 482->528 (matches your _BNS_MAP).

new.title: Trial / judgment in absence of proclaimed offender (in absentia)

mappings.summary: NEW. Enables trial and judgment in absentia against a proclaimed offender who has absconded to evade trial and where there is no immediate prospect of arrest. No CrPC counterpart.
Definitions renumbered 3->2. 'Document' and 'evidence' now expressly include electronic and digital records.
Renumbered 8->6.

old.title: Confession caused by inducement, threat or promise - irrelevant

new.title: Confession caused by inducement, threat, coercion or promise - irrelevant

mappings.summary: Renumbered 24->22. 'Coercion' added alongside inducement/threat/promise.

old.title: Confession to police officer not provable

new.title: Confession to police officer not provable

mappings.summary: Renumbered 25->23(1). Bar on confessions to police retained.

old.title: How much of information received from accused may be proved (discovery)

mappings.summary: The discovery exception (old s.27) is now the proviso to BSA 23(2). Your _BNS_MAP's '27 -> 23' is correct at the section level; precisely it is 23(2) proviso.

old.title: Statements by persons who are dead or cannot be found (dying declaration)

new.title: Statements by persons who are dead or cannot be found (dying declaration)

mappings.summary: Dying declaration renumbered 32->26.
Renumbered 45->39.

Renumbered 65B->63 (matches your _BNS_MAP). The certificate requirement is retained and the prescribed certificate format is now in the Schedule to BSA; scope expanded to a wider range of digital/electronic records.
Renumbered 101->104.
Renumbered 113B->118. (113A abetment-of-suicide presumption -> BSA 117.)
Renumbered 118->124.

old.title: Cross-examination as to previous statements in writing

new.title: Cross-examination as to previous statements in writing

mappings.summary: Renumbered 145->148.
